In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 45L of the Reserve Bank of India
Act, 1934 and all other provisions / laws enabling the Reserve Bank of India ('RBI') in
this regard, RBI being satisfied that it is necessary and expedient in the public interest
so to do, hereby, issues the Directions hereinafter specified.

children.title: A. Short Title and Commencement
children.text: A. Short Title and Commencement

children.title: 1. These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial
children.text:
1. These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial
Companies - Managing Risks in Outsourcing) Directions, 2025.

children.title: 2. These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
children.text:
2. These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
Provided that for Non-Banking Financial Companies covered under the scope of
these Directions, as mentioned in paragraph 3, their existing Information Technology
(IT) outsourcing agreements regardless of whether they are due for renewal on or
after the effective date of these Directions shall comply with the provisions of these
Directions either at the time of renewal or by April 10, 2026, whichever is earlier.
However, their new IT outsourcing agreements that come into force on or after the
effective date of these Directions, shall comply with the provisions of these
Directions from the date of agreement itself.
Provided further that, nothing in the preceding proviso shall be construed as
permitting non-compliance with any other extant regulatory instructions or statutory
requirements applicable to such arrangements.

children.title: B. Applicability and Scope
children.text: B. Applicability and Scope

children.title: 3. (1) These Directions shall be applicable to following Non-Banking Financial
children.text:

Companies, (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'NBFCs' and individually as an
NBFC), subject to layer-wise applicability:

children.title: (xi) NBFC-AA registered with the RBI under the provisions of the RBI Act, 1934.
children.text:
(xi) NBFC-AA registered with the RBI under the provisions of the RBI Act, 1934.
(2) The provisions prescribed in these directions except Chapter IV and IT-specific
provisions contained in Chapter II (e.g., Board-level IT outsourcing policy and
responsibilities of and reviews by the Board with respect to IT outsourcing) of
these Directions shall apply to NBFCs included in Base layer. These directions
including Chapter IV and IT-specific provisions contained in Chapter II are
applicable for NBFCs in Middle layer and above layers.
(3) The paragraphs contained in Chapter IV and IT-specific paragraphs contained
in Chapter II are applicable for an MGC registered with RBI under the scheme of
Registration of Mortgage Guarantee Companies, categorized in Middle layer and
above layers.
(4) These Directions are not applicable for NOFHC registered with the RBI as
NBFC under the provisions of the RBI Act, 1934.

children.title: Note: The applicability under these Directions is in line with the regulatory
children.text:
Note: The applicability under these Directions is in line with the regulatory
structure for NBFCs as set out in Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial
Companies - Registration, Exemptions and Framework for Scale Based
Regulation) Directions, 2025.

children.title: 4. The scope of provisions contained in Chapter III shall be as under:
children.text:
4. The scope of provisions contained in Chapter III shall be as under:
(1) The provisions shall apply to material outsourcing arrangements entered into by
an NBFC with a service provider which may be either a member of the group /
conglomerate to which the NBFC belongs, or an unrelated party which is located
in India or elsewhere for outsourcing of financial services like applications

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
processing (loan origination, credit card), document processing, marketing and
research, supervision of loans, data processing and back office related activities.
Provided that for outsourced services relating to credit cards, the provisions set
out in the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Credit
Cards: Issuance and Conduct) Directions, 2025, as amended from time to time,
shall also apply to the applicable entities.
(2) The provisions shall not apply to outsourcing of:

children.title: (i) IT services as defined in paragraph 58(1) of these Directions, unless
children.text:
(i) IT services as defined in paragraph 58(1) of these Directions, unless
specified otherwise in respective paragraphs of Chapter IV; and

children.title: (ii) activities unrelated to financial services like usage of courier, catering of
children.text:
(ii) activities unrelated to financial services like usage of courier, catering of
staff, housekeeping and janitorial services, security of the premises,
movement and archiving of records, etc.

children.title: 5. The scope of provisions contained in Chapter IV shall be as under:
children.text:
5. The scope of provisions contained in Chapter IV shall be as under:

defined in paragraph 58(2) above. In this context, 'Outsourcing of IT Services'
shall include outsourcing of the following services:

children.text:

children.title: (iv) services and operations related to data centres;
children.text: (iv) services and operations related to data centres;

children.text: (v) cloud computing services;

children.text: (vi) managed security services; and

children.text:

(2) The provisions shall not apply to the following services / activities,

children.title: (i) corporate internet banking services obtained by an NBFC as a corporate
children.text:
(i) corporate internet banking services obtained by an NBFC as a corporate
customer or sub-member of another Regulated Entity (RE);
Provided that for the purpose of these Directions, the following indicative
(but not exhaustive) list of entities shall be considered as REs -
Commercial Banks; Local Area Banks; Small Finance Banks; Payments
Banks; Regional Rural Banks; other NBFCs in Base Layer (NBFC - BL),
Middle Layer (NBFC - ML), and Upper Layer (NBFC - UL); All India
Financial Institutions; Credit Information Companies; Urban Co-operative
Banks; Rural Co-operative Banks; and Payment System Operators,
For the purpose of these Directions, 'Commercial Banks' means banking
companies (other than Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks,
Payments Banks and Regional Rural Banks), corresponding new banks,
and the State Bank of India as defined respectively under clauses (c),
(da), and (nc) of section 5 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.

children.title: (ii) external audit services such as Vulnerability Assessment (VA) /
children.text:
(ii) external audit services such as Vulnerability Assessment (VA) /
Penetration Testing (PT), Information Systems Audit, and security review;

children.title: (iii) SMS gateways (including bulk SMS service providers);
children.text: (iii) SMS gateways (including bulk SMS service providers);

children.text: (iv) procurement of IT hardware or appliances;

children.title: (ix) any other off-the-shelf products (e.g., anti-virus software, and email
(x) services obtained by an NBFC as a sub-member of a Centralised
children.text:

children.title: C. Definitions
children.text:
C. Definitions
6.
In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires,
(1) 'Outsourcing' means use of a third party (either an affiliated entity within a
corporate group or an entity that is external to the corporate group) by an NBFC to
perform activities on a continuing basis that would normally be undertaken by the
NBFC itself, now or in the future. 'Continuing basis' shall include agreements for a
limited period.
(2) 'Group' shall be as defined in the Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Banks -
Concentration Risk Management) Directions, 2025, as amended from time to time,
for the purpose of intragroup transactions and exposures;
7.
Some other terms pertaining to outsourcing of financial services and IT services
have been defined in their respective Chapters as per their applicability.
8.
All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as have
been assigned to them under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or the
Information Technology Act, 2000 or the Companies Act, 2013 and Rules made

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
thereunder, or any statutory modification or re-enactment thereto, or Glossary of
Terms published by RBI or as used in commercial parlance, as the case may be.

hierarchy.title: Chapter II - Role of the Board
hierarchy.text:
Chapter II - Role of the Board
9.
The outsourcing of any activity by an NBFC does not diminish its obligations, and
those of its Board and Senior Management, who have the ultimate responsibility
for the outsourced activity.

children.title: A. Board Approved Policy
children.text: A. Board Approved Policy

children.title: 10. An NBFC intending to outsource any of its financial or IT activities shall put in
children.text:
10. An NBFC intending to outsource any of its financial or IT activities shall put in
place corresponding comprehensive Board approved outsourcing policy, the
coverage of which is indicated in paragraph 20 and paragraph 62, respectively.

children.title: B. Key responsibilities
children.text: B. Key responsibilities

children.title: 11. The Board or a Committee of the Board to which powers have been delegated, as
children.text:
11. The Board or a Committee of the Board to which powers have been delegated, as
applicable for financial or IT outsourcing, shall be responsible for putting in place a
framework to evaluate the risks and materiality of all existing and prospective
outsourcing arrangements, laying down appropriate approval authorities
depending on risks and materiality, and undertaking regular review.

children.title: 12. In respect of outsourcing of financial services,
children.text:
12. In respect of outsourcing of financial services,
(1) the Board, or a Committee of the Board to which powers have been
delegated, shall be responsible, inter alia, for:

children.title: (i) approving a framework to evaluate the risks and materiality of all
(ii) laying down appropriate approval authorities for outsourcing depending
children.text:
(ii) laying down appropriate approval authorities for outsourcing depending
on risks and materiality;

children.title: (iii) setting up suitable administrative framework of Senior Management;
children.text: (iii) setting up suitable administrative framework of Senior Management;

children.title: (iv) undertaking regular review of outsourcing strategies and arrangements
children.text:
(iv) undertaking regular review of outsourcing strategies and arrangements
for their continued relevance, and safety and soundness;

children.title: (v) deciding on business activities of a material nature to be outsourced,
children.text:
(v) deciding on business activities of a material nature to be outsourced,
and approving such arrangements;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (vi) approving a policy for outsourcing financial services to a group entity;
children.text:
(vi) approving a policy for outsourcing financial services to a group entity;
and

children.title: (vii) reviewing records of all material outsourcing on half yearly basis, in
children.text:
(vii) reviewing records of all material outsourcing on half yearly basis, in
which case the delegation should be to Risk Management Committee
only.
(2) the Audit Committee of the Board (ACB) of an NBFC shall:

children.title: (i) monitor the system of internal audit of all outsourced activities; and
children.text: (i) monitor the system of internal audit of all outsourced activities; and

children.title: (ii) review the ageing analysis of entries pending reconciliation with
children.text:
(ii) review the ageing analysis of entries pending reconciliation with
outsourced vendors and make efforts to reduce the old outstanding
items therein at the earliest.

children.title: 13. In respect of outsourcing of IT services, the Board shall be responsible, inter alia,
children.text:
13. In respect of outsourcing of IT services, the Board shall be responsible, inter alia,
for:

children.title: (i) putting in place a framework for approval of outsourcing activities depending
children.text:
(i) putting in place a framework for approval of outsourcing activities depending
on risks and materiality;

children.title: (ii) approving policies to evaluate the risks and materiality of all existing and
children.text:
(ii) approving policies to evaluate the risks and materiality of all existing and

children.title: (iii) setting up suitable administrative framework of Senior Management;
children.text: (iii) setting up suitable administrative framework of Senior Management;

children.title: (iv) ensuring either by itself or through its Committee that there is no conflict of
children.text:
(iv) ensuring either by itself or through its Committee that there is no conflict of
interest arising out of third-party engagements, especially when permitting an

if not a group company, shall not be owned or controlled by any director, key
managerial personnel, approver of the outsourcing arrangement, or their
relatives, as indicated under the proviso to paragraph 59 of these Directions;
and

children.title: (v) reviewing any adverse development mentioned in reports put up to Senior

hierarchy.title: Chapter III - Outsourcing of Financial Services
hierarchy.text: Chapter III - Outsourcing of Financial Services

children.title: A. Definitions
children.text: A. Definitions

children.title: 14. In this Chapter, unless the context otherwise requires, 'Material Outsourcing'
children.text:
14. In this Chapter, unless the context otherwise requires, 'Material Outsourcing'
means arrangements, which if disrupted, have the potential to significantly impact
the business operations, reputation or profitability or customer service. 'Materiality'
of outsourcing shall be based on the:

children.title: (i) level of importance to the NBFC of the activity being outsourced as well as
children.text:
(i) level of importance to the NBFC of the activity being outsourced as well as
the significance of the risk posed by the same;

children.title: (ii) potential impact of the outsourcing on the NBFC on various parameters such
children.text:
(ii) potential impact of the outsourcing on the NBFC on various parameters such
as earnings, solvency, liquidity, funding, capital, and risk profile;

children.title: (iii) likely impact on the NBFC's reputation and brand value, and ability to
(iv) cost of the outsourcing as a proportion of total operating costs of the NBFC;
children.text: (iv) cost of the outsourcing as a proportion of total operating costs of the NBFC;

children.title: (v) aggregate exposure to that particular service provider, in cases where the
children.text:
(v) aggregate exposure to that particular service provider, in cases where the
NBFC outsources various functions to the same service provider; and

children.title: (vi) significance of activities outsourced in the context of customer service and
children.text:
(vi) significance of activities outsourced in the context of customer service and
protection.

children.title: B. Activities that shall not be outsourced
children.text: B. Activities that shall not be outsourced

children.title: 15. An NBFC which chooses to outsource financial services shall however not
children.text:
15. An NBFC which chooses to outsource financial services shall however not
outsource core management functions including Internal Audit, strategic and
compliance functions, and decision-making functions such as determining
compliance with KYC norms for opening deposit accounts, giving sanction for
loans (including retail loans) and management of investment portfolio.
Provided that for an NBFC in a group / conglomerate, these functions may be
outsourced within the group subject to compliance with instructions in paragraph
45 to paragraph 51.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: Explanation: While internal audit function itself is a management process, the
children.text:
Explanation: While internal audit function itself is a management process, the
internal auditors can be on contract.

children.title: C. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight
children.text: C. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight

children.title: 16. An NBFC, which desires to outsource financial services, shall not require prior
children.text:
16. An NBFC, which desires to outsource financial services, shall not require prior
approval from RBI. However, such arrangements would be subject to on-site / off-
site monitoring and inspection / scrutiny by RBI.

children.title: 17. As stated in paragraph 9, the outsourcing of any activity by an NBFC shall not
children.text:
17. As stated in paragraph 9, the outsourcing of any activity by an NBFC shall not
diminish its obligations including to its customers and RBI, and those of its Board
and senior management, who have the ultimate responsibility for the outsourced
activity. The NBFC shall, therefore, be responsible for the actions of its service
provider including Direct Sales Agents (DSAs) / Direct Marketing Agents (DMAs)
and recovery agents and the confidentiality of information pertaining to the
customers that is available with the service provider. The NBFC shall retain
ultimate control of the outsourced activity.

children.title: 18. An NBFC shall ensure that:
children.text: 18. An NBFC shall ensure that:

children.title: (i) all relevant laws, regulations, rules, guidelines and conditions of approval,
(ii) outsourcing, whether the service provider is located in India or outside, does
children.text:
(ii) outsourcing, whether the service provider is located in India or outside, does
not impede RBI in carrying out its supervisory functions and objectives, or
diminish the ability of an NBFC to fulfil its obligations to the regulator /
supervisor;

children.title: (iii) outsourcing, whether the service provider is located in India or outside, does
(iv) outsourcing would not result in the compromise or weakening of an NBFC's
children.text:
(iv) outsourcing would not result in the compromise or weakening of an NBFC's
internal control, business conduct, or reputation;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) the service provider employs the same high standard of care in performing
children.text:
(v) the service provider employs the same high standard of care in performing
the services as would be employed by the NBFC, if the activities were
conducted within the NBFC and not outsourced; and

children.title: (vi) the service provider, if not a group company of the NBFC, shall not be owned
children.text:
(vi) the service provider, if not a group company of the NBFC, shall not be owned
or controlled by any director of the NBFC, or their relatives having the same
meaning as assigned under Companies Act, 2013 and the Rules framed
thereunder, as amended from time to time.

children.title: 19. An NBFC shall be responsible for making Currency Transactions Reports (CTRs)
children.text:
19. An NBFC shall be responsible for making Currency Transactions Reports (CTRs)
and Suspicious Transactions Reports (STRs) to FIU or any other competent
authority in respect of its customer related activities carried out by the service
providers.

children.title: D. Governance Framework
children.text:
D. Governance Framework
D.1 Outsourcing Policy

children.title: 20. An NBFC intending to outsource any of its financial services shall put in place a
children.text:
20. An NBFC intending to outsource any of its financial services shall put in place a
comprehensive outsourcing policy, approved by its Board, which shall incorporate,
inter alia, the following:

children.title: (i) criteria for selection of such activities and service providers;
children.text: (i) criteria for selection of such activities and service providers;

children.title: (ii) delegation of authority depending on risks, and materiality; and
children.text: (ii) delegation of authority depending on risks, and materiality; and

children.title: (iii) systems to monitor and review these activities.
children.text:
(iii) systems to monitor and review these activities.
D.2 Role of Senior Management

children.title: 21. The Senior Management of an NBFC shall, inter alia, be responsible for:
children.text: 21. The Senior Management of an NBFC shall, inter alia, be responsible for:

children.title: (i) evaluating the risks and materiality of all existing and prospective
(ii) developing and implementing sound and prudent outsourcing policies and
<image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (iii) reviewing periodically the effectiveness of policies and procedures;
children.text: (iii) reviewing periodically the effectiveness of policies and procedures;

children.title: (iv) communicating information pertaining to material outsourcing risks to the
children.text:
(iv) communicating information pertaining to material outsourcing risks to the
Board in a timely manner;

children.title: (v) ensuring that contingency plans, based on realistic and probable disruptive
children.text:
(v) ensuring that contingency plans, based on realistic and probable disruptive
scenarios, are in place and tested;

children.title: (vi) ensuring that there is independent review and audit for compliance with set
children.text:
(vi) ensuring that there is independent review and audit for compliance with set
policies; and

children.title: (vii) undertaking periodic review of outsourcing arrangements to identify new
children.text:
(vii) undertaking periodic review of outsourcing arrangements to identify new
material outsourcing risks as they arise.

children.title: E. Risk Management
children.text:
E. Risk Management
E.1 Evaluation of the Risks

children.title: 22. An NBFC shall evaluate and guard against the following key risks when entering
children.text:
22. An NBFC shall evaluate and guard against the following key risks when entering
into outsourcing arrangement:

children.title: (i) Strategic Risk - such as where the service provider conducts business on
children.text:
(i) Strategic Risk - such as where the service provider conducts business on
its own behalf, inconsistent with the overall strategic goals of the NBFC.

children.title: (ii) Reputation Risk - such as where the service provider delivers poor service,
children.text:
(ii) Reputation Risk - such as where the service provider delivers poor service,
or its customer interactions are inconsistent with the overall standards of the
NBFC.

children.title: (iii) Compliance Risk - such as where, owing to outsourcing, the privacy,
children.text:
(iii) Compliance Risk - such as where, owing to outsourcing, the privacy,
consumer, and prudential laws are not adequately complied with.

children.title: (iv) Operational Risk - which may arise due to technology failure, fraud, error,
children.text:
(iv) Operational Risk - which may arise due to technology failure, fraud, error,
or inadequate financial capacity of the service provider to fulfil obligations or
to provide remedies.

children.title: (v) Legal Risk - where an NBFC is subjected to, inter alia, fines, penalties, or
children.text:
(v) Legal Risk - where an NBFC is subjected to, inter alia, fines, penalties, or
punitive damages resulting from supervisory actions, or private settlements
due to omissions and commissions by the service provider.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (vi) Exit Strategy Risk - may arise when an NBFC becomes over reliant on one
children.text:
(vi) Exit Strategy Risk - may arise when an NBFC becomes over reliant on one
service provider, loses relevant internal skills preventing it from bringing the
activity back in-house, or enters into contracts that make speedy exits
prohibitively expensive.

children.title: (vii) Counterparty Risk - such as where the service provider engages in
children.text:
(vii) Counterparty Risk - such as where the service provider engages in
inappropriate underwriting or credit assessments.

children.title: (viii) Country Risk - where the political, social, or legal climate creates added risk
children.text:
(viii) Country Risk - where the political, social, or legal climate creates added risk
in the outsourcing arrangement.

children.title: (ix) Contractual Risk - where the NBFC may not have the ability to enforce the
children.text:
(ix) Contractual Risk - where the NBFC may not have the ability to enforce the
contract with the service provider.

children.title: (x) Concentration and Systemic Risk - where there is a lack of control of an
children.text:
(x) Concentration and Systemic Risk - where there is a lack of control of an
NBFC over a service provider, more so when overall industry has
considerable exposure to one service provider.
E.2 Confidentiality and Security of Information

children.title: 23. An NBFC shall seek to ensure the confidentiality, security, preservation, and
children.text:
23. An NBFC shall seek to ensure the confidentiality, security, preservation, and
protection of the customer information in the custody or possession of the service
provider.

children.title: 24. Access to customer information by a service provider or its staff shall be on a
children.text:
24. Access to customer information by a service provider or its staff shall be on a
'need to know' basis, i.e., limited to those areas where the information is required
in order to perform the outsourced function.

children.title: 25. An NBFC shall review and monitor the security practices and control processes of
children.text:
25. An NBFC shall review and monitor the security practices and control processes of
its service providers on a regular basis and require the service provider to disclose
security breaches.

children.title: 26. In instances, where a service provider acts as an outsourcing agent for multiple
children.text:
26. In instances, where a service provider acts as an outsourcing agent for multiple
entities, the NBFC shall take care to build strong safeguards so that there is no
comingling or combining of information, documents, records, and assets.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: 27. An NBFC shall ensure that a service provider is able to isolate and clearly identify
children.text:
27. An NBFC shall ensure that a service provider is able to isolate and clearly identify
the NBFC's customer information, documents, records and assets to protect the
confidentiality of the information.

children.title: 28. An NBFC shall immediately notify RBI in the event of breach of security and
children.text:
28. An NBFC shall immediately notify RBI in the event of breach of security and
leakage of confidential customer related information. In these eventualities, the
NBFC shall be liable to its customers for any damage.

children.title: F. Outsourcing Process
children.text:
F. Outsourcing Process
F.1 Service Provider Evaluation

children.title: 29. An NBFC shall perform appropriate due diligence while considering or renewing an
children.text:
29. An NBFC shall perform appropriate due diligence while considering or renewing an
outsourcing arrangement, to assess the capability of the service provider to
comply with obligations in the outsourcing agreement on an ongoing basis.

children.title: 30. The due diligence mentioned above shall involve an evaluation of all available
children.text:
30. The due diligence mentioned above shall involve an evaluation of all available
information, as applicable, about the service provider, including but not limited to:

children.title: (i) qualitative, quantitative, financial, operational, legal, and reputational factors;
children.text: (i) qualitative, quantitative, financial, operational, legal, and reputational factors;

children.title: (ii) risks arising from undue concentration, if outsourcing to a single service
children.text:
(ii) risks arising from undue concentration, if outsourcing to a single service
provider, or a limited number of service providers;

children.title: (iii) past experience, and demonstrated competence to implement and support
children.text:
(iii) past experience, and demonstrated competence to implement and support
the proposed activity over the contracted period;

children.title: (iv) financial soundness and ability to service commitments even under adverse
children.text:
(iv) financial soundness and ability to service commitments even under adverse
conditions;

children.title: (v) business reputation and culture, compliance, complaints and outstanding or
children.text:
(v) business reputation and culture, compliance, complaints and outstanding or
potential litigation;

children.title: (vi) quality of due diligence exercised by the service provider of its employees
children.text:
(vi) quality of due diligence exercised by the service provider of its employees
and sub-contractors;

children.title: (vii) security and internal control, audit coverage, reporting, and monitoring
children.text:
(vii) security and internal control, audit coverage, reporting, and monitoring
procedures, and business continuity management; and

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (viii) external factors like political, economic, social, and legal environment of the
children.text:
(viii) external factors like political, economic, social, and legal environment of the
jurisdiction in which the service provider operates and other events that may
impact data security and service performance.

children.title: 31. Where possible, an NBFC shall obtain independent reviews and market feedback
children.text:
31. Where possible, an NBFC shall obtain independent reviews and market feedback
on the service provider to supplement the findings of its own due diligence.

children.title: 32. An NBFC shall also evaluate whether the systems of its service provider are
children.text:
32. An NBFC shall also evaluate whether the systems of its service provider are
compatible with those of the NBFC, and the acceptability of their standards of
performance including in the area of customer service.
F.2 Outsourcing Agreement

children.title: 33. An NBFC shall ensure that the terms and conditions governing the outsourcing
children.text:
33. An NBFC shall ensure that the terms and conditions governing the outsourcing
arrangement are carefully defined in written agreements and vetted by the NBFC's
legal counsel on their legal effect and enforceability. The agreement shall
appropriately reckon the associated risks and the strategies for mitigating or
managing them. The NBFC shall ensure that such an agreement is sufficiently
flexible to allow it to retain an appropriate level of control over the outsourcing and
the right to intervene with appropriate measures to meet legal and regulatory
obligations. The agreement shall also bring out the nature of legal relationship
between the parties, i.e., whether agent-principal or otherwise.

children.title: 34. Some of the key provisions of the agreement shall include:
children.text: 34. Some of the key provisions of the agreement shall include:

children.title: (i) details of the activity being outsourced, including appropriate service and
children.text:
(i) details of the activity being outsourced, including appropriate service and
performance standards;

children.title: (ii) NBFC's access to all books, records, and information relevant to the
children.text:
(ii) NBFC's access to all books, records, and information relevant to the
outsourced activity available with the service provider;

children.title: (iii) regular and continuous monitoring and assessment by the NBFC of the
(iv) prior approval or consent by the NBFC for the use of subcontractors by the
children.text:
(iv) prior approval or consent by the NBFC for the use of subcontractors by the
service provider for all or part of an outsourced activity;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) controls for maintaining confidentiality of data including of its customers, and
(vi) contingency plans to ensure business continuity;
children.text: (vi) contingency plans to ensure business continuity;

children.title: (vii) NBFC's right to conduct audits on the service provider whether by its internal
children.text:
(vii) NBFC's right to conduct audits on the service provider whether by its internal
or external auditors, or by agents appointed to act on its behalf and to obtain
copies of any audit or review reports and findings made on the service
provider in conjunction with the services performed for the NBFC;

children.title: (viii) right of RBI or persons authorised by it to access the NBFC's documents,
(ix) right of RBI to cause an inspection to be made of the service provider of the
(x) a termination clause and minimum period for executing termination, if
children.text:
(x) a termination clause and minimum period for executing termination, if
deemed necessary;

children.title: (xi) provision that confidentiality of customers' information shall be maintained
children.text:
(xi) provision that confidentiality of customers' information shall be maintained
even after the contract expires or gets terminated; and

children.title: (xii) provisions to ensure that the service provider preserves documents and data
children.text:
(xii) provisions to ensure that the service provider preserves documents and data
in accordance with legal and regulatory obligations of the NBFC and take
suitable steps to ensure that its interests are protected in this regard even
post termination of the services.
F.3 Monitoring and Control of Outsourced Activities

children.title: 35. An NBFC shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
children.text:
35. An NBFC shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
outsourced activities and shall ensure that outsourcing agreements with its service
provider contain provisions to address the same.

children.title: 36. An NBFC shall maintain a central record of all material outsourcing of financial
children.text:
36. An NBFC shall maintain a central record of all material outsourcing of financial
services for review by its Board and Senior Management. The records shall be

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
updated promptly, and half yearly reviews shall be placed before the Board or Risk
Management Committee.

children.title: 37. Regular audits, by either the internal auditors or external auditors of an NBFC shall
children.text:
37. Regular audits, by either the internal auditors or external auditors of an NBFC shall
assess the adequacy of the risk management practices adopted in overseeing and
managing the outsourcing arrangement, the NBFC's compliance with its risk
management framework, and the requirements of these Directions.

children.title: 38. An NBFC shall, at least on an annual basis, review the financial and operational
children.text:
38. An NBFC shall, at least on an annual basis, review the financial and operational
condition of the service provider to assess its ability to continue to meet its
outsourcing obligations. Such due diligence reviews, which shall be based on all
available information about the service provider, shall highlight any deterioration or
breach in performance standards, confidentiality, and security, and in operational
resilience or business continuity preparedness.

children.title: 39. Certain services, viz., outsourcing of cash management, might involve
children.text:
39. Certain services, viz., outsourcing of cash management, might involve
reconciliation of transaction between an NBFC, and the service provider (or its
subcontractors). In such cases, the NBFC shall ensure that reconciliation of
transactions between itself and the service provider (or its subcontractors) is
carried out in a timely manner.
F.4 Business Continuity and Management of Disaster Recovery Plan

children.title: 40. An NBFC shall require its service provider to develop and establish a robust
children.text:
40. An NBFC shall require its service provider to develop and establish a robust
framework for documenting, maintaining, and testing business continuity and
recovery procedures. The NBFC shall ensure that the service provider periodically
tests the Business Continuity and Recovery Plan and may also consider
occasional joint testing and recovery exercises with its service provider.

children.title: 41. In establishing a viable contingency plan, an NBFC shall consider the availability of
children.text:
41. In establishing a viable contingency plan, an NBFC shall consider the availability of
alternative service providers or the possibility of bringing the outsourced activity
back in-house in an emergency, and the costs, time, and resources that would be
involved.

children.title: 42. An NBFC shall ensure that its service providers are able to isolate the NBFC's
children.text:
42. An NBFC shall ensure that its service providers are able to isolate the NBFC's
information, documents, and records, and other assets so that in adverse

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
conditions or termination of the agreement, all documents, records of transactions
and information given to the service provider, and assets of the NBFC, can be
removed from the possession of the service provider (in order to continue its
business operations); or deleted, destroyed or rendered unusable.

children.title: 43. In order to mitigate the risk of unexpected termination of the outsourcing
children.text:
43. In order to mitigate the risk of unexpected termination of the outsourcing
agreement or insolvency or liquidation of its service provider, an NBFC shall retain
an appropriate level of control over its outsourcing arrangement along with the
right to intervene with appropriate measures to continue its business operations
without incurring prohibitive expenses and disruption in the operations of the
NBFC and its services to the customers.
F.5 Termination

children.title: 44. In the event of termination of an outsourcing agreement for any reason, this shall
children.text:
44. In the event of termination of an outsourcing agreement for any reason, this shall
be publicised by the NBFC by displaying at a prominent place in the branch,
posting it on the website, and informing the customers so as to ensure that the
customers do not continue to deal with the service provider.

children.title: G. Specific Outsourcing Arrangements
children.text:
G. Specific Outsourcing Arrangements
G.1 Outsourcing within a Group / Conglomerate
45.
In a group structure, an NBFC may have back-office and service arrangements or
agreements with group entities such as sharing of premises, legal and other
professional services, hardware and software applications and centralized back-
office functions; outsourcing certain financial services to other group entities.
Before entering into such arrangements with group entities, an NBFC shall have a
policy approved by the Board or a Committee of the Board and also Service Level
Agreements (SLAs) with its group entities, which shall also cover demarcation of
sharing resources, e.g., premises, and personnel.
46.
In such arrangements, an NBFC shall inform the customers specifically about the
company which is actually offering the product / service, wherever there are
multiple group entities involved, or any cross selling observed.
47.
While entering into such arrangements, an NBFC shall ensure that these:

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (i) are appropriately documented in written agreements with details like scope
(ii) do not lead to any confusion to the customers on whose products /
children.text:
(ii) do not lead to any confusion to the customers on whose products /
services they are availing by clear physical demarcation of the space
where the activities of the NBFC and those of its group entities are
undertaken;

children.title: (iii) do not compromise the ability of the NBFC to identify and manage risk on
children.text:
(iii) do not compromise the ability of the NBFC to identify and manage risk on
a stand-alone basis;

children.title: (iv) do not prevent RBI from being able to obtain information required for the
children.text:
(iv) do not prevent RBI from being able to obtain information required for the
supervision of the NBFC or pertaining to the group as a whole; and

children.title: (v) incorporate a clause under the written agreements that there is a clear
children.text:
(v) incorporate a clause under the written agreements that there is a clear
obligation for any service provider to comply with directions given by RBI
in relation to the activities of the NBFC.
48.
An NBFC shall ensure that its ability to carry out its operations in a sound fashion
would not be affected, if premises or other services (such as IT systems and
support staff) provided by the group entities become unavailable.
49.
If the premises of an NBFC are shared with the group entities for the purpose of
cross-selling, the NBFC shall take measures to ensure that the entity's
identification is distinctly visible and clear to the customers. The marketing
brochure used by the group entity and verbal communication by its staff / agent in
the NBFC's premises shall mention nature of arrangement of the entity with the
NBFC so that the customers are clear on the seller of the product.
50.
An NBFC shall not publish any advertisement or enter into any agreement stating
or suggesting or giving tacit impression that it is in any way responsible for the
obligations of its group entities.

children.title: 51. The risk management practices to be adopted by an NBFC while outsourcing to a
children.text:
51. The risk management practices to be adopted by an NBFC while outsourcing to a
related party (i.e., party within the group / conglomerate) shall be identical to those
for a non-related party.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
G.2 Offshore outsourcing

children.title: 52. In principle, outsourcing arrangements shall only be entered into with parties
children.text:
52. In principle, outsourcing arrangements shall only be entered into with parties
operating in jurisdictions that uphold confidentiality agreements and clauses.

children.title: 53. While engaging with service provider(s) in a foreign country, an NBFC shall:
children.text: 53. While engaging with service provider(s) in a foreign country, an NBFC shall:

children.title: (i) closely monitor government policies of the jurisdiction in which the service
children.text:
(i) closely monitor government policies of the jurisdiction in which the service
provider is based and political, social, economic, and legal conditions, both
during the risk assessment and on a continuous basis, and establish sound
procedures for dealing with country risk. This includes having appropriate
contingency and exit strategies;

children.title: (ii) clearly specify the governing law of the outsourcing arrangement;
children.text: (ii) clearly specify the governing law of the outsourcing arrangement;

children.title: (iii) ensure that the availability of records to the NBFC and RBI will not be
(iv) ensure activities outsourced outside India are conducted in a manner so as
(v) ensure that, where the offshore service provider is a regulated entity, the
(vi) ensure that the regulatory authority of the offshore location does not have
(vii) ensure that the jurisdiction of the courts in the offshore location where data is
children.text:
(vii) ensure that the jurisdiction of the courts in the offshore location where data is
maintained does not extend to the operations of the NBFC in India on the
strength of the fact that the data is being processed there even though the
actual transactions are undertaken in India; and

children.title: (viii) ensure that all original records continue to be maintained in India.
children.text: (viii) ensure that all original records continue to be maintained in India.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: H. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services
children.text: H. Redressal of Grievances related to Outsourced Services

children.title: 54. Outsourcing arrangements entered into by an NBFC shall not affect the rights of its
children.text:
54. Outsourcing arrangements entered into by an NBFC shall not affect the rights of its
customers against the NBFC, including the ability of the customers to obtain
redressal as applicable under relevant laws.

children.title: 55. In cases where customers are required to deal with a service provider in the
children.text:
55. In cases where customers are required to deal with a service provider in the
process of dealing with an NBFC, the NBFC shall incorporate a clause in the
corresponding product literature and brochures, stating that services of the service
provider, including in sales, and marketing of the products, may be used. The role
of the service provider may be indicated in broad terms.

children.title: 56. An NBFC shall have a robust grievance redressal mechanism that shall not be
children.text:
56. An NBFC shall have a robust grievance redressal mechanism that shall not be
compromised in any manner on account of outsourcing, i.e., responsibility for
redressal of customers' grievances related to outsourced services shall rest with
the NBFC. In case of microfinance loans, a declaration that, (i) the NBFC shall be
accountable for inappropriate behaviour of the employees of the service provider
and (ii) shall provide timely grievance redressal, shall be made in the loan
agreement, and Fair Practices Code (FPC) displayed in its office, branch premises
and its website.

children.title: 57. In addition to the above,
children.text:
57. In addition to the above,
(1) an NBFC shall constitute Grievance Redressal Machinery in accordance with
the provisions of Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies -
Responsible Business Conduct) Directions, 2025;
(2) at the operational level, an NBFC shall display the name and contact details
(Telephone / Mobile Nos. as also email address) of the Grievance Redressal
Officer prominently at its branches / places where business is transacted. The
designated officer shall ensure that genuine grievances of customers are
redressed promptly without involving delay. It shall be clearly indicated that
NBFC's Grievance Redressal Machinery will also deal with the issue relating to
services provided by the outsourced agency;

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(3) an NBFC shall give a time limit of 30 days to the customers for preferring their
complaints or grievances. The grievance redressal procedure of the NBFC and
the time frame fixed for responding to the complaints shall be placed on the
NBFC's website; and
(4) if a complaint is rejected wholly or partly by an NBFC and the complainant is
not satisfied with the reply or does not get any reply within 30 days, after the
NBFC received the complaint, the complainant shall have the following options
for redressal of their grievance(s):

children.title: (i) the RBI's Ombudsman in case of NBFCs to which RBI's Integrated
children.text:
(i) the RBI's Ombudsman in case of NBFCs to which RBI's Integrated
Ombudsman Scheme, 2021 applies, or

children.title: (ii) Consumer Education and Protection Cell (CEPC) of respective Regional
children.text:
(ii) Consumer Education and Protection Cell (CEPC) of respective Regional
Office of RBI in case of NBFCs to which RBI's Integrated Ombudsman
Scheme, 2021 does not apply.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Chapter IV - Outsourcing of Information Technology (IT) Services
hierarchy.text: Chapter IV - Outsourcing of Information Technology (IT) Services

children.title: A. Definitions
children.text: A. Definitions

children.title: 58. In this Chapter, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall bear
children.text:
58. In this Chapter, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall bear
the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'IT services' means IT services / IT enabled services / IT activities.
(2) 'Material Outsourcing of IT Services' are those which:

children.title: (i) if disrupted or compromised shall have the potential to significantly
children.text:
(i) if disrupted or compromised shall have the potential to significantly
impact the NBFC's business operations; or

children.title: (ii) may have material impact on the NBFC's customers in the event of
children.text:
(ii) may have material impact on the NBFC's customers in the event of
any unauthorised access, loss, or theft of customer information;
(3) 'Service Provider' means provider of IT services including entities related to
the NBFC, or those which belong to the same group or conglomerate to which
the NBFC belongs.
Provided that for the purpose of this Chapter, the following indicative (but not
exhaustive) list of vendors and entities shall not be considered as 'Service
Providers' as defined above:

children.title: (i) vendors providing business services using IT (e.g., BCs);
children.text: (i) vendors providing business services using IT (e.g., BCs);

children.title: (ii) Payment System Operators (PSOs) authorised by RBI under the Payment
(iii) partnership based FinTech firms such as those providing co-branded
(iv) FinTech firms providing services for data retrieval, and data validation and
children.text:
(iv) FinTech firms providing services for data retrieval, and data validation and
verification such as, financial statement analysis, GST returns analysis,
fetching of vehicle information, digital document execution, data entry and
call centre services;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) telecom service providers from whom leased lines or other similar kind of
children.text:
(v) telecom service providers from whom leased lines or other similar kind of
infrastructure are availed and used for transmission of data; and

children.title: (vi) security or audit consultants appointed for certification, audit or VA / PT
children.text:
(vi) security or audit consultants appointed for certification, audit or VA / PT
related to IT infra, IT services, or Information Security services in their role
as independent third-party auditor, consultant, lead implementer.

children.title: Explanation: In case an IT service is provided by an RE to an NBFC (as
children.text:
Explanation: In case an IT service is provided by an RE to an NBFC (as
applicable to the scope of Directions under this Chapter), even the RE
could be considered as a service provider of the NBFC, under this
Chapter.
(4) 'Sub-contractor' refers to those providing material / significant IT services to
the service provider and is specific to the material / significant IT services
arrangement that the NBFC has entered into with the service provider.

children.title: B. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight
children.text: B. Authorisation, Accountability, and Oversight

children.title: 59. In addition to adhering to the requirements stipulated in subparagraphs (i) to (v) of
children.text:
59. In addition to adhering to the requirements stipulated in subparagraphs (i) to (v) of
paragraph 18 of these Directions, an NBFC shall ensure that the service provider,
if not a group company, shall not be owned or controlled by any director, or key
managerial personnel, or approver of the outsourcing arrangement of the NBFC,
or their relatives. The terms 'control', 'director', 'key managerial personnel', and
'relative' shall have the same meaning as assigned under the Companies Act,
2013 and the Rules framed thereunder from time to time.
Provided that an exception to the above requirement may be made with the
approval of Board or a Committee of the Board, followed by appropriate
disclosure, oversight and monitoring of such arrangements.

children.title: 60. An NBFC shall evaluate the need for outsourcing of IT services based on a
children.text:
60. An NBFC shall evaluate the need for outsourcing of IT services based on a
comprehensive assessment of attendant benefits, risks, and availability of
commensurate processes to manage those risks. For this purpose, the NBFC
shall, inter alia, consider the following:

children.title: (i) the need for outsourcing based on materiality / criticality of activity to be
children.text:
(i) the need for outsourcing based on materiality / criticality of activity to be
outsourced;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) expectations and outcomes from outsourcing;
children.text: (ii) expectations and outcomes from outsourcing;

children.title: (iii) success factors and cost-benefit analysis; and
children.text: (iii) success factors and cost-benefit analysis; and

children.title: (iv) the model for outsourcing.
children.text: (iv) the model for outsourcing.

children.title: 61. An NBFC shall ensure that cyber incidents are reported to it by the service
children.text:
61. An NBFC shall ensure that cyber incidents are reported to it by the service
provider without undue delay, so that an incident is reported by the NBFC to the
RBI within six hours of detection by the service provider.

children.title: C. Governance Framework
children.text:
C. Governance Framework
C.1 Outsourcing Policy

children.title: 62. An NBFC intending to outsource any of its IT services shall put in place a
children.text:
62. An NBFC intending to outsource any of its IT services shall put in place a
comprehensive Board approved IT outsourcing policy incorporating, inter alia, the
following:

children.title: (i) the roles and responsibilities of the Board, Committees of the Board (if any)
(ii) criteria for selection of such services and service providers;
children.text: (ii) criteria for selection of such services and service providers;

children.title: (iii) parameters for defining material outsourcing based on the broad criteria
children.text:
(iii) parameters for defining material outsourcing based on the broad criteria
defined in paragraph 58(2) of these Directions;

children.title: (iv) delegation of authority depending on risk and materiality;
children.text: (iv) delegation of authority depending on risk and materiality;

children.title: (v) disaster recovery and business continuity plans;
children.text: (v) disaster recovery and business continuity plans;

children.title: (vi) systems to monitor and review the operations of these services; and
children.text: (vi) systems to monitor and review the operations of these services; and

children.title: (vii) termination processes and exit strategies, including business continuity in the
children.text:
(vii) termination processes and exit strategies, including business continuity in the
event of a service provider exiting the outsourcing arrangement.
C.2 Role of Senior Management

children.title: 63. The Senior Management of an NBFC shall, inter alia, be responsible for:
children.text: 63. The Senior Management of an NBFC shall, inter alia, be responsible for:

children.title: (i) formulating IT outsourcing policies and procedures, evaluating the risks and
children.text:
(i) formulating IT outsourcing policies and procedures, evaluating the risks and
materiality of all existing and prospective IT outsourcing arrangements based
on the framework commensurate with the complexity, nature and scope, in

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
line with the enterprise-wide risk management of the NBFC approved by the
Board and its implementation;

children.title: (ii) prior evaluation of prospective IT outsourcing arrangements and periodic
children.text:
(ii) prior evaluation of prospective IT outsourcing arrangements and periodic
evaluation of the existing outsourcing arrangements covering the
performance review, criticality and associated risks of all such arrangements
based on the policy approved by the Board;

children.title: (iii) identifying IT outsourcing risks as they arise, monitoring, mitigating,
(iv) ensuring that suitable business continuity plans based on realistic and
(v) ensuring (a) effective oversight over the service provider (and its
(vi) ensuring an independent review and audit on a periodic basis for compliance
children.text:
(vi) ensuring an independent review and audit on a periodic basis for compliance
with the legislations, regulations, Board-approved policy, and performance
standards, and reporting the same to Board or a Committee of the Board;
and

children.title: (vii) creating essential capacity with required skillsets within the NBFC for proper
children.text:
(vii) creating essential capacity with required skillsets within the NBFC for proper
oversight of outsourced services.
C.3 Role of IT Function

children.title: 64. The responsibilities of the IT Function of an NBFC shall, inter alia, include:
<image>
children.text:
<image>
(3) effectively monitoring and supervising the outsourced IT activity to ensure
that the service provider meets the laid down performance standards and
provides uninterrupted services, reporting to the Senior Management, co-
ordinating periodic due diligence, and highlighting concerns, if any; and
(4) putting in place necessary documentation required for contractual
agreements including service level management, monitoring of vendor
operations, key risk indicators, and classifying the vendors as per the
determined risk.

children.title: D. Risk Management
children.text:
D. Risk Management
D.1 Risk Management Framework

children.title: 65. An NBFC shall put in place a risk management framework that comprehensively
children.text:
65. An NBFC shall put in place a risk management framework that comprehensively
deals with the processes and responsibilities for identification, measurement,
mitigation, management, and reporting of risks associated with such IT
outsourcing arrangements.

children.title: 66. An NBFC shall suitably document risk assessments with necessary approvals in
children.text:
66. An NBFC shall suitably document risk assessments with necessary approvals in
line with the roles and responsibilities of the Board of Directors, Senior
Management, and IT Function and subject the same to internal and external
quality assurance on a periodic basis as determined by the Board-approved policy.

children.title: 67. An NBFC shall effectively assess the impact of concentration risk posed by
children.text:
67. An NBFC shall effectively assess the impact of concentration risk posed by
multiple outsourcing arrangements to the same service provider and the
concentration risk posed by outsourcing critical or material functions to a limited
number of service providers.
D.2 Confidentiality and Security of Information

children.title: 68. An NBFC shall be responsible for the confidentiality and integrity of data and
children.text:
68. An NBFC shall be responsible for the confidentiality and integrity of data and
information pertaining to its customers that are available to the service provider.

children.title: 69. In this regard, an NBFC shall adhere to directions stated in paragraph 23 to
children.text:
69. In this regard, an NBFC shall adhere to directions stated in paragraph 23 to
paragraph 26 of these Directions and additionally ensure that:

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (i) access by service providers to data at the NBFC, or its data centre shall be
(ii) in the event of multiple service provider relationships where two or more
children.text:
(ii) in the event of multiple service provider relationships where two or more
service providers collaborate to deliver an end-to-end solution, the NBFC
remains responsible for understanding and monitoring the control
environment of all service providers that have access to its data, systems,
records, or resources;

children.title: (iii) it immediately notifies RBI in the event of breach of security and leakage of
children.text:
(iii) it immediately notifies RBI in the event of breach of security and leakage of
confidential customer-related information. In these eventualities, the NBFC
shall adhere to the extant instructions issued by RBI from time to time on
Incident Response and Recovery Management.

children.title: 70. With regard to requirement that the data should not be combined or comingled, as
children.text:
70. With regard to requirement that the data should not be combined or comingled, as
stipulated in paragraph 26, it would suffice if there is clear separation and isolation
of data (NBFC, and its customer specific data and information) to ensure that only
the personnel as authorised by the NBFC is able to access data that belongs to
them in a multi-tenant environment / architecture.

children.title: E. Outsourcing Process
children.text:
E. Outsourcing Process
E.1 Service Provider Evaluation

children.title: 71. The directions regarding service provider evaluation as applicable to outsourcing
children.text:
71. The directions regarding service provider evaluation as applicable to outsourcing
of financial services contained in paragraph 29 to paragraph 31 shall apply,
mutatis mutandis, to outsourcing of IT services, with the following additional
considerations:

children.title: (i) technology, infrastructural stability, data backup arrangements, and disaster
children.text:
(i) technology, infrastructural stability, data backup arrangements, and disaster
recovery plan;

children.title: (ii) conflict of interest, if any;
children.text: (ii) conflict of interest, if any;

children.title: (iii) capability to identify, and segregate NBFC's data;
children.text: (iii) capability to identify, and segregate NBFC's data;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (iv) capability to comply with the regulatory and legal requirements of the
children.text:
(iv) capability to comply with the regulatory and legal requirements of the
outsourcing arrangement;

children.title: (v) information / cyber security risk assessment;
children.text: (v) information / cyber security risk assessment;

children.title: (vi) ensuring that appropriate controls, assurance requirements, and possible
children.text:
(vi) ensuring that appropriate controls, assurance requirements, and possible
contractual arrangements are in place to ensure data protection and NBFC's
access to the data which is processed, managed or stored by the service
provider;

children.title: (vii) ability to effectively service all the customers while maintaining confidentiality,
children.text:
(vii) ability to effectively service all the customers while maintaining confidentiality,
especially where a service provider has exposure to multiple entities; and

children.title: (viii) ability to enforce agreements and the rights available thereunder including
children.text:
(viii) ability to enforce agreements and the rights available thereunder including
those relating to aspects such as data storage, data protection, and
confidentiality.

children.title: 72. An NBFC should adopt a risk-based approach in conducting such due diligence
children.text:
72. An NBFC should adopt a risk-based approach in conducting such due diligence
activities.
E.2 Outsourcing Agreement

children.title: 73. An NBFC shall ensure that its rights and obligations and those of each service
children.text:
73. An NBFC shall ensure that its rights and obligations and those of each service
provider are clearly defined and set out in a legally binding written agreement, in
line with the provisions specified in paragraph 33 of these Directions. In principle,
the provisions of the agreement shall appropriately reckon the criticality of the
outsourced task to the business of the NBFC, the associated risks and the
strategies for mitigating or managing them.

children.title: 74. In addition to the requirements specified in subparagraphs (i) to (vii) of paragraph
children.text:
74. In addition to the requirements specified in subparagraphs (i) to (vii) of paragraph
34, an NBFC shall also include at minimum (as applicable to the scope of
Directions in this Chapter) the following aspects in any agreement for outsourcing
of IT services:

children.title: (i) provisions covering service provider's subcontractors with respect to
<image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) access by the NBFC to all data, books, records, information logs, alerts
(iii) type of material adverse events (e.g., data breaches, denial of service, and
children.text:
(iii) type of material adverse events (e.g., data breaches, denial of service, and
service unavailability, relevant to the outsourced service) and the incidents
required to be reported to the NBFC to enable the NBFC to take prompt
risk mitigation measures, and ensure compliance with statutory and
regulatory guidelines;

children.title: (iv) compliance with the provisions of Information Technology Act, 2000, and
(v) the deliverables including SLAs formalising performance criteria to
children.text:
(v) the deliverables including SLAs formalising performance criteria to
measure the quality and quantity of service levels;

children.title: (vi) storage of data only in India (as applicable) as per extant regulatory
children.text:
(vi) storage of data only in India (as applicable) as per extant regulatory
requirements;

children.title: (vii) clauses requiring the service provider to provide details of data (related to
children.text:
(vii) clauses requiring the service provider to provide details of data (related to
the NBFC and its customers) captured, processed, and stored;

children.title: (viii) types of data / information that the service provider (vendor) is permitted to
children.text:
(viii) types of data / information that the service provider (vendor) is permitted to
share with NBFC's customer and / or any other party;

children.title: (ix) the resolution process, events of default, indemnities, remedies, and
children.text:
(ix) the resolution process, events of default, indemnities, remedies, and
recourse available to the respective parties;

children.title: (x) contingency plan(s) to ensure testing requirements;
children.text: (x) contingency plan(s) to ensure testing requirements;

children.title: (xi) right to seek information from the service provider about the third parties
children.text:
(xi) right to seek information from the service provider about the third parties
(in the supply chain) engaged by the former;

children.title: (xii) right of RBI or person(s) authorised by it to perform inspection of the
children.text:
(xii) right of RBI or person(s) authorised by it to perform inspection of the
service provider and any of its sub-contractors and access the NBFC's IT
infrastructure, applications, data, documents, and other necessary
information given to, stored or processed by the service provider and its

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
sub-contractors, in relation and as applicable to the scope of the
outsourcing arrangement;

children.title: (xiii) clauses making the service provider contractually liable for the
children.text:
(xiii) clauses making the service provider contractually liable for the
performance and risk management practices of its subcontractors;

children.title: (xiv) obligation of the service provider to comply with directions issued by the
(xv) termination rights of the NBFC, including the ability to orderly transfer the
(xvi) obligation of the service provider to co-operate with the relevant authorities
children.text:
(xvi) obligation of the service provider to co-operate with the relevant authorities
in case of insolvency or resolution of the NBFC;

children.title: (xvii) provision to consider skilled resources of service provider who provide
children.text:
(xvii) provision to consider skilled resources of service provider who provide
core services as 'essential personnel' so that a limited number of staff with
back-up arrangements necessary to operate critical functions can work on-
site during exigencies (including pandemic situations);

children.title: (xviii) clause requiring suitable back-to-back arrangements between service
children.text:
(xviii) clause requiring suitable back-to-back arrangements between service
providers and the OEMs; and

children.title: (xix) clause requiring non-disclosure agreement with respect to information
children.text:
(xix) clause requiring non-disclosure agreement with respect to information
retained by the service provider.
E.3 Monitoring and Control of Outsourced Activities

children.title: 75. An NBFC shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
children.text:
75. An NBFC shall have in place a management structure to monitor and control its
outsourced services. This shall include (as applicable to the scope of Directions in
this Chapter), but not be limited to, monitoring the performance, uptime of the
systems and resources, service availability, adherence to SLA requirements, and
incident response mechanism.

children.title: 76. An NBFC shall conduct regular audits (as applicable to the scope of Directions in
children.text:
76. An NBFC shall conduct regular audits (as applicable to the scope of Directions in
this Chapter), of service providers (including sub-contractors) with regard to the

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
service outsourced by it. Such audits may be conducted either by NBFC's internal
or external auditors appointed to act on NBFC's behalf.

children.title: 77. While outsourcing various IT services, more than one RE may be availing services
children.text:
77. While outsourcing various IT services, more than one RE may be availing services
from the same third-party service provider. In such scenarios, in lieu of conducting
separate audits by individual REs of the common service provider, they may adopt
pooled (shared) audit. This allows the relevant REs to either pool their audit
resources or engage an independent third-party auditor to jointly audit a common
service provider. However, in doing so, it shall be the responsibility of REs in
ensuring that the audit requirements related to their respective contract with the
service provider are met effectively.

children.title: 78. The audits shall assess, inter alia, the performance of the service provider,
children.text:
78. The audits shall assess, inter alia, the performance of the service provider,
adequacy of the risk management practices adopted by the service provider, and
compliance with laws and regulations. The frequency of the audit shall be
determined based on the nature and extent of risk and impact on the NBFC from
the outsourcing arrangements. Reports on the monitoring and control activities
shall be reviewed periodically by the Senior Management, and in case of any
adverse development, the same shall be put up to the Board for information.

children.title: 79. An NBFC, depending upon the risk assessment, may also rely upon globally
children.text:
79. An NBFC, depending upon the risk assessment, may also rely upon globally
recognised third-party certifications made available by the service provider in lieu
of conducting independent audits. However, this shall not absolve the NBFC of its
responsibility in ensuring assurance on the controls and procedures required to
safeguard data security (including availability of systems) at the service provider's
end.

children.title: 80. An NBFC shall periodically review the financial and operational condition of the
children.text:
80. An NBFC shall periodically review the financial and operational condition of the
service provider to assess its ability to continue to meet its obligations. An NBFC
shall adopt risk-based approach in defining the periodicity. Such due diligence
reviews shall highlight any deterioration or breach in performance standards,
confidentiality security, and operational resilience preparedness.

children.title: 81. An NBFC shall ensure that the service provider grants unrestricted and effective
children.text:
81. An NBFC shall ensure that the service provider grants unrestricted and effective
access to (a) data related to the outsourced services; (b) the relevant business

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
premises of the service provider; subject to appropriate security protocols, for the
purpose of effective oversight by the NBFC, its auditors, RBI and other relevant
Competent Authorities, as authorised under law.
E.4 Inventory of Outsourced Services

children.title: 82. An NBFC shall create an inventory of IT services outsourced to service providers
children.text:
82. An NBFC shall create an inventory of IT services outsourced to service providers
(including key entities involved in their supply chains). Further, the NBFC shall
map its dependency on third parties and periodically evaluate the information
received from the service providers.
E.5 Business Continuity and Management of Disaster Recovery Plan

children.title: 83. The Directions regarding 'Business Continuity and Management of Disaster
children.text:
83. The Directions regarding 'Business Continuity and Management of Disaster
Recovery Plan' as applicable to outsourcing of financial services contained in
paragraph 40 to paragraph 43 shall apply, mutatis mutandis, to outsourcing of IT
services. The Business Continuity Plan (BCP) and Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP)
for outsourced IT services shall be commensurate with the nature and scope of the
outsourced service as per extant instructions issued by RBI from time to time on
BCP / DR requirements.
E.6 Exit Strategy

children.title: 84. The IT outsourcing policy shall contain a clear exit strategy for ensuring business
children.text:
84. The IT outsourcing policy shall contain a clear exit strategy for ensuring business
continuity during and after exit.

children.title: 85. The strategy shall include plans for different scenarios of exit or termination of
children.text:
85. The strategy shall include plans for different scenarios of exit or termination of
services with stipulation of minimum period to execute such plans, as necessary.

children.title: 86. In documenting its exit strategy, an NBFC shall, inter alia, identify alternative
children.text:
86. In documenting its exit strategy, an NBFC shall, inter alia, identify alternative
arrangements, which may include performing the service by a different service
provider, or by the NBFC itself.

children.title: 87. An NBFC shall ensure that outsourcing agreements have necessary clauses on
children.text:
87. An NBFC shall ensure that outsourcing agreements have necessary clauses on
safe removal or destruction of data, hardware and all records (digital and physical),
as applicable. Further, the outsourcing agreement shall ensure that the service
provider is prohibited from erasing, purging, revoking, altering, or changing any

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
data during the transition period, unless specifically advised by the regulator or the
concerned NBFC.

children.title: 88. A service provider shall be legally obliged to cooperate fully with both the NBFC
children.text:
88. A service provider shall be legally obliged to cooperate fully with both the NBFC
and its new service provider(s) to ensure there is a smooth transition.
E.7 Termination

children.title: 89. In the event of termination of the outsourcing agreement for any reason in cases
children.text:
89. In the event of termination of the outsourcing agreement for any reason in cases
where the service provider deals with the customers of the NBFC, the same shall
be given due publicity by the NBFC so as to ensure that the customers stop
dealing with the concerned service provider.

children.title: F. Specific Outsourcing Arrangements
children.text:
F. Specific Outsourcing Arrangements
F.1 Outsourcing within a Group / Conglomerate

children.title: 90. An NBFC may outsource any IT service within its business group / conglomerate,
children.text:
90. An NBFC may outsource any IT service within its business group / conglomerate,
provided that such an arrangement is backed by the Board-approved policy, and
appropriate SLAs / agreements with its group entities are in place.

children.title: 91. The selection of a group entity shall be based on objective reasons that are similar
children.text:
91. The selection of a group entity shall be based on objective reasons that are similar
to selection of a third-party, and the NBFC shall appropriately deal with any
conflicts of interest that such an outsourcing arrangement may entail.

children.title: 92. An NBFC, at all times, shall maintain an arm's length relationship in dealings with
children.text:
92. An NBFC, at all times, shall maintain an arm's length relationship in dealings with
its group entities. Risk management practices being adopted by the NBFC while
outsourcing to a group entity shall be identical to those specified for a non-related
party.
F.2 Offshore or Cross-Border outsourcing

children.title: 93. In principle, outsourcing arrangements shall only be entered into with parties
children.text:
93. In principle, outsourcing arrangements shall only be entered into with parties
operating in jurisdictions that uphold confidentiality clauses and agreements.

children.title: 94. While engaging with service provider(s) in a foreign country, an NBFC shall:
children.text: 94. While engaging with service provider(s) in a foreign country, an NBFC shall:

children.title: (i) closely monitor government policies of the jurisdiction in which the service
children.text:
(i) closely monitor government policies of the jurisdiction in which the service
provider is based and the political, social, economic and legal conditions on a
continuous basis, and establish sound procedures for mitigating the country

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
risk. This includes, inter alia, having appropriate contingency and exit
strategies;

children.title: (ii) clearly specify the governing law of the outsourcing arrangement;
children.text: (ii) clearly specify the governing law of the outsourcing arrangement;

children.title: (iii) ensure that availability of records to the NBFC and the RBI will not be
children.text:
(iii) ensure that availability of records to the NBFC and the RBI will not be
affected even in case of liquidation of the service provider;

children.title: (iv) ensure the right of the NBFC and RBI to direct and conduct audit or
children.text:
(iv) ensure the right of the NBFC and RBI to direct and conduct audit or
inspection of the service provider based in a foreign jurisdiction; and

children.title: (v) ensure that the arrangement complies with all statutory requirements as well
children.text:
(v) ensure that the arrangement complies with all statutory requirements as well
as regulations issued by the RBI from time to time.
F.3 Outsourcing of Security Operations Centre (SOC)

children.title: 95. Considering the risks associated with outsourcing of Security Operations Centre
children.text:
95. Considering the risks associated with outsourcing of Security Operations Centre
(SOC) operations by an NBFC, such as data being stored and processed at an
external location and managed by the service provider (or its subcontractors) to
which the NBFC has lesser visibility, the NBFC, to mitigate the risks, shall adopt
the following requirements in the case of outsourcing of SOC operations in
addition to the controls prescribed in this Chapter:

children.title: (i) unambiguously identify the owner of assets used in providing the services
children.text:
(i) unambiguously identify the owner of assets used in providing the services
(e.g., systems, software, source code, processes, and concepts);
